description_long: This is a classical-style marble sculpture depicting a draped human figure standing upright on a rectangular plinth. The figure is headless and armless, with intricately carved folds of fabric cascading down the body, suggesting a toga or robe. The front reveals detailed drapery and subtle anatomical contours, while the back shows less detail and some discoloration or weathering. The sculpture's surface is predominantly white with areas of brown and reddish staining, likely from age or exposure. The base is simple and unadorned, serving to elevate the figure. The overall impression is of an ancient or neoclassical work, possibly representing a mythological or historical subject.
description: A headless, armless marble sculpture of a draped human figure, standing on a rectangular base with detailed folds in the garment and visible weathering.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed drapery and the missing head and arms, with the folds of the garment clearly visible.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the depth of the drapery and the rounded shoulder, with less detail on the garment's folds.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals discoloration and weathering, with less intricate detail in the drapery and a broad back.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the garment's folds and the side of the plinth, with some reddish staining visible on the upper section.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
